statement: There exists an injective but not surjective linear endomorphism of `Real^Nat`.
lean_statement:
theorem exists_injective_not_surjective_endomorphism_real_sequences :
    ∃ f : (Nat → Real) →ₗ[Real] (Nat → Real),
      Function.Injective f ∧ ¬ Function.Surjective f
proof:
Proof: take the right shift `f` defined by `(f x) 0 = 0` and `(f x) (n+1) = x n`. This map is
linear. It is injective: if `f x = f y`, then for each `n` we have `x n = (f x) (n+1) = (f y) (n+1)
 = y n`, so `x = y`. It is not surjective because every element in the image has `0` as its first
coordinate, so for example the sequence with first coordinate `1` is not in the image.
